root_cause.text: An overly permissive bounds check on the `map_length` value in the RLE image coder allows a shift operation (`one << map_length`) to overflow or produce an excessively large value, leading to a massive memory allocation request that fails.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `coders/rle.c` changes the bounds check for `map_length` from `map_length >= 64` to `map_length >= 32` right before the left shift operation `map_length = one << map_length;`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: An overly permissive bounds check on the `map_length` value in the RLE image coder allows a shift operation (`one << map_length`) to overflow or produce an excessively large value, leading to a massive memory allocation request that fails.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `coders/rle.c` changes the bounds check for `map_length` from `map_length >= 64` to `map_length >= 32` right before the left shift operation `map_length = one << map_length;`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Lower the maximum allowed map_length to 31 to prevent undefined behavior during a bitwise shift.
intent_record.what: Changed the threshold for throwing an 'ImproperImageHeader' exception from map_length >= 64 to map_length >= 32.
intent_record.how: Restricts map_length to a maximum of 31, preventing undefined behavior or overflow when it is used as the shift amount in 'one << map_length'.

grouping_result.intent: Lower the maximum allowed map_length to 31 to prevent undefined behavior during a bitwise shift.

relevance_review.reason: The group's core intent directly addresses the vulnerability root cause by lowering the maximum allowed map_length to 31, preventing the shift operation from overflowing and causing massive memory allocation requests.

candidates.rationale: This is the overly permissive bounds check that allows map_length values up to 63, which causes the subsequent shift operation to overflow or produce an excessively large value.
This shift operation overflows or produces an excessively large value due to the inadequate bounds check on map_length.

vuln_statements.explanation: This overly permissive bounds check allows map_length to be large enough to cause undefined behavior or massive allocation when shifted.
This is the shift operation that overflows or produces an excessively large value due to the inadequate bounds check.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit caf07b0f71a915d00c6667efd90a138340735eb7
Author: cristy <urban-warrior@git.imagemagick.org>
Date:   Sat May 24 23:26:25 2014 +0000

diff --git a/coders/rle.c b/coders/rle.c
index a1fb82069..24b44f00e 100644
--- a/coders/rle.c
+++ b/coders/rle.c
@@ -219,8 +219,11 @@ static Image *ReadRLEImage(const ImageInfo *image_info,ExceptionInfo *exception)
     number_planes=1UL*ReadBlobByte(image);
     bits_per_pixel=1UL*ReadBlobByte(image);
     number_colormaps=1UL*ReadBlobByte(image);
+    map_l
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces a bounds check 'if (map_length >= 64)' before performing the shift operation 'map_length = one << map_length', which prevents the overflow described in the root cause. Thus, the fix is present in this commit.
